name: Pellorneidae
description: Pellorneidae, the ground babblers, are a family of passerine birds found in tropical Asia and Africa. They are primarily terrestrial birds that forage on the forest floor, feeding on insects and other invertebrates.

children.name: Pellorneum
children.description: A genus of terrestrial babblers with streaked or spotted underparts, found in Asian forests.

children.name: Pellorneum ruficeps
children.description: The Puff-throated Babbler is a brownish babbler with heavily streaked white underparts and a rufous crown. It is a skulking, terrestrial bird found on the forest floor across South and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Pellorneum malaccense
children.description: The Short-tailed Babbler is a stocky, short-tailed babbler with plain brown upperparts and whitish underparts with a buff wash on the breast. It skulks on the forest floor of lowland rainforests in the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. Its song is a series of clear, descending whistles.

children.name: Pellorneum rostratum
children.description: The White-chested Babbler has a distinctive white throat and breast contrasting with brown upperparts and a dark crown. It inhabits lowland forests and swampy areas of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. It forages on the ground for insects, often in pairs.

children.name: Pellorneum tickelli
children.description: The Buff-breasted Babbler is a warm brown babbler with a buff-coloured breast and a faintly streaked throat. It is found in hill forests from the eastern Himalayas through mainland Southeast Asia. It is a shy, terrestrial bird that keeps to dense undergrowth.

children.name: Pellorneum pyrrogenys
children.description: Temminck's Babbler has a rufous crown, olive-brown upperparts, and whitish underparts with heavy dark streaking. It is found in lowland and hill forests of Java, Borneo, and Sumatra. It forages on the forest floor, turning over leaves in search of invertebrates.

children.name: Pellorneum bicolor
children.description: The Ferruginous Babbler is a striking babbler with rich rufous-brown upperparts, a grey head, and white underparts. It inhabits lowland and foothill forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. It is an active forager, often seen in mixed-species flocks on the forest floor.

children.name: Malacocincla
children.description: A genus of medium-sized, plain-colored ground babblers found in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Malacocincla abbotti
children.description: Abbott's Babbler is a plain brown bird with a whitish throat and a short tail. It skulks in dense undergrowth in lowland forests across Southeast Asia, foraging for insects on the ground.

children.name: Napothera
children.description: A genus of small, short-tailed babblers that inhabit dense forest undergrowth in Asia.

children.name: Napothera epilepidota
children.description: The Streaked Wren-babbler is a tiny babbler with dark scaled and streaked plumage, resembling a wren. It forages in dense understory and bamboo thickets from the Himalayas to Sumatra.

children.name: Malacopteron
children.description: A genus of medium-sized babblers with rufous or chestnut crowns, found in Southeast Asian forests.

children.name: Malacopteron cinereum
children.description: The Scaly-crowned Babbler has a chestnut crown with scaly markings, grey upperparts, and pale underparts. It forages in the mid-story of lowland rainforests across Southeast Asia.

children.name: Kenopia
children.description: A monotypic genus of wren-babbler with striking black-and-white striped plumage, found in Southeast Asian forests.

children.name: Kenopia striata
children.description: The Striped Wren-babbler is a striking babbler with bold black-and-white stripes on the head and upperparts. It inhabits lowland forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. It forages on or near the ground in dense undergrowth.

children.name: Turdinus
children.description: A genus of large wren-babblers with stout bills and thrush-like proportions, found in Southeast Asian forests.

children.name: Turdinus macrodactylus
children.description: The Large Wren-babbler is a large, dark babbler with a stout bill and a short tail, found in montane forests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Java. It has dark brown upperparts, a grey throat, and heavily scaled underparts. It skulks in dense understory, foraging for insects on the ground.

children.name: Turdinus atrigularis
children.description: The Black-throated Wren-babbler has a distinctive black throat and upper breast, a white belly, and dark brown upperparts. It is endemic to the montane forests of Borneo. It forages on the forest floor in small groups.

children.name: Gypsophila
children.description: A genus of wren-babblers adapted to limestone karst habitats in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Gypsophila crassa
children.description: The Mountain Wren-babbler is a large, dark wren-babbler with a stout bill and heavily scaled underparts, found in montane forests of Sumatra, Borneo, and the Malay Peninsula. It forages on the forest floor for insects. Its loud, ringing song is a characteristic sound of highland forests.

children.name: Laticilla
children.description: A genus of long-tailed, grassland-dwelling babblers found in South Asia. They inhabit tall grasslands and reed beds, where they skulk low in dense vegetation.

children.name: Laticilla burnesii
children.description: The Rufous-vented Grass Babbler is a small, long-tailed babbler with streaked brown upperparts and a distinctive rufous vent. It inhabits tall grasslands and reed beds in Pakistan and northwestern India. It is a skulking, secretive bird that is more often heard than seen.

children.name: Laticilla cinerascens
children.description: The Swamp Grass Babbler is a dull brown babbler with a pale throat and belly, found in swamp grasslands of the Brahmaputra floodplains of India and Bangladesh. It is a shy, difficult-to-see bird that keeps to dense, tall grasses. Its habitat is threatened by drainage and conversion to agriculture.
